summary: A community-driven registry of 28 contributed projects built on Palantir AIP, showcasing SDKs, workflows, and applications that integrate with Foundry's Ontology, Compute Modules, and AI Platform. Projects are distributed as Marketplace packages (.zip bundles) containing Ontology definitions, backing datasets, Actions, Functions, and optional OSDK-powered React frontends. The repository serves as the primary showcase for how external developers interact with Foundry's Ontology through the OSDK client library, demonstrating Object Type querying, Action invocation, aggregation, pagination, media handling, derived properties, and real-time event streaming patterns.

ontologyConcepts.evidence: Projects define domain-specific Object Types backed by datasets: Whales, WhaleActivity (Tailbook), Song, Playlist (Foundry for Tunes), Hospital, MerReportDate, HawaiiObject, SuppliesDataset (HADR-Aid), Email, EmailAttachment, CalendarEvent, CalendarEventAttendee (Gmail Connector), Field Extraction Job (Feedback Loop). Object Types are imported from generated SDK packages (e.g., '@whaletail/sdk', '@relar/sdk', '@hadr-aid/sdk') and accessed via `client(ObjectType)` pattern. Each SDK exports `$Objects` enumeration of all available Object Types.

Object properties are typed fields on OSDK instances. Whale objects have: commonName, locality, waterBody, scientificName, verbatimLocality, externalResource, organismId, organismName, vernacularName, speciesName, higherGeography, occurrenceRemarks, distinctOrganismIdCount. Song objects have: userId, deleted, lastPlayedAt, createdAt, liked, likedAt, playedCount, content (media), duration, albumArtist, albumName, artist, genre, trackNumber, year. The OSDK uses `$primaryKey` and `$title` as special meta-properties. Properties support filtering with operators like `$eq`, `$gt`.

Playlist objects link to Song objects via `songsIds` and `songEntryIds` arrays, representing a many-to-many link between Playlists and Songs. The Platform Governance app models links between Groups, Users (Principals), Resources, and ResourceRoles - a group has many group members, resources connect to principals via resource roles. The Feedback Loop project uses links between Field Extraction Jobs and their source documents.

Actions are invoked via `client(ActionDef).applyAction({params})` pattern. Foundry for Tunes defines: deleteSong, logSongPlayed, setLiked, editSongMetadata, createPlaylist, addSongToPlaylist, removeSongFromPlaylist, renamePlaylist, deletePlaylist. Tailbook defines: createWhaleActivity (with `$returnEdits: true` to get created objects back). Gmail Connector defines: SendEmail, CreateEvent. Each SDK exports `$Actions` enumeration. Actions support optimistic updates with undo patterns via `$updateInternalValues`.

Each generated SDK exports `$Queries` alongside `$Objects` and `$Actions`, representing Ontology Functions (server-side query definitions). The Feedback Loop project uses AIP Logic functions backed by the Ontology for entity extraction workflows, with AIP Evals evaluation suites testing these functions. Compute Modules (e.g., Gmail send, Geocoding) provide server-side function execution with defined input/output schemas using TypeBox.

Platform Governance App explicitly models access control: resources connect to groups/users via ResourceRoles with roles (owner, editor, viewer, discoverer). The app surfaces inherited permissions through group hierarchies. Service users authenticate via OAuth with client ID/secret stored in REST sources. CORS configuration required for OSDK access. The 'Everyone' principal type represents all stack users. Linter alerts flag when 'Everyone' has editor/owner access.

Multiple integration patterns demonstrated: (1) External transforms pull data from Google APIs via Data Connection REST sources with OAuth credentials, (2) Compute Modules run containerized code (Docker) for bidirectional integrations like Gmail sending and geocoding, (3) Push-Based Events via Data Connection listeners accept webhooks from systems like Jira Cloud into streaming datasets, (4) Pipeline Builder transforms create Ontology-backing datasets, (5) Platform SDK pulls Foundry metadata (users, groups, resources) into datasets via code repositories.

AIP (AI Platform) integration shown across multiple projects: (1) Feedback Loop uses LLM-powered entity extraction from insurance claims with AIP Logic functions, user feedback captured in Ontology, and AIP Evals for evaluation suites, (2) WLED Controller uses AIP/LLMs for automated holiday detection to drive IoT effects, (3) Meal Planning uses Computer Vision for fridge photo analysis, (4) Fashion Assistant uses CV for wardrobe analysis, (5) Gmail Connector requires GPT-4o mini and text-embedding-ada-002 model mappings, (6) Small Business Connector leverages AIP semantics for business matching.

The OSDK uses `ObjectOrInterfaceDefinition` from '@osdk/api' as the base type for both Object Types and Interfaces, with `Osdk.Instance<O>` providing typed instances. The `ObjectSet<O>` generic enables polymorphic querying. Workshop widgets use iframe-based custom widget interfaces via '@osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget' with `IConfigDefinition` defining typed fields (string, number, boolean, date, timestamp, objectSet, lists, events) for bidirectional Workshop-widget communication.

apiSurface.name: createClient
apiSurface.description: Creates an OSDK Client instance from '@osdk/client'. Takes foundryUrl, $ontologyRid (from generated SDK), and an OAuth auth object. Returns a Client that is callable as `client(ObjectType)` to create ObjectSets.

apiSurface.name: createPublicOauthClient
apiSurface.description: Creates OAuth authentication from '@osdk/oauth'. Takes clientId, foundryUrl, and redirectUrl. Used for browser-based public OAuth flows with Foundry.

apiSurface.name: $ontologyRid
apiSurface.description: Auto-generated Ontology Resource Identifier exported from each project's SDK package (e.g., '@whaletail/sdk', '@relar/sdk'). Required to initialize the OSDK client.

apiSurface.name: client(ObjectType).where(filter)
apiSurface.description: Creates a filtered ObjectSet. Filter syntax: `{ property: value }` for equality, `{ property: { $eq: value } }` for explicit equality, `{ property: { $gt: value } }` for greater-than. Chainable with .fetchPage(), .asyncIter(), .aggregate().

apiSurface.name: ObjectSet.fetchPage
apiSurface.description: Fetches a page of objects from an ObjectSet. Takes `{ $pageSize: number, $nextPageToken?: string }`. Returns `{ data: Instance[], nextPageToken?: string }`. Used for paginated object retrieval.

apiSurface.name: ObjectSet.asyncIter
apiSurface.description: Returns an async iterator over all objects in an ObjectSet. Used with `for await (const obj of objectSet.asyncIter())` pattern. Handles pagination transparently.

apiSurface.name: ObjectSet.aggregate
apiSurface.description: Performs aggregations on ObjectSets. Supports `$select: { $count: 'unordered' }` for counts and `$groupBy: { property: 'exact' }` for grouping. Returns array of `{ $group: {...}, $count: number }` or single `{ $count: number }`.

apiSurface.name: client(ActionType).applyAction
apiSurface.description: Invokes an Ontology Action. Takes action parameters as first argument and optional config `{ $returnEdits: true }` as second. When returnEdits is true, returns `{ type: 'edits', editedObjectTypes: [...] }`. Actions can create, modify, or delete objects.

apiSurface.name: $Objects / $Actions / $Queries
apiSurface.description: Generated SDK packages export these constants enumerating all available Object Types, Action Types, and Query definitions in the Ontology. Used to discover available API surface at runtime.

apiSurface.name: Osdk.Instance<O>
apiSurface.description: Generic type from '@osdk/client' representing a typed instance of an Object Type or Interface. Provides strongly-typed access to all properties. Has special properties: $primaryKey, $title. Includes $updateInternalValues for optimistic updates.

apiSurface.name: ObjectSet<O>
apiSurface.description: Generic type from '@osdk/client' representing a set of objects that can be filtered, paginated, iterated, or aggregated. Created by calling `client(ObjectType)` and optionally chaining `.where()`.

apiSurface.name: ComputeModule
apiSurface.description: From '@palantir/compute-module'. Configures compute modules with sources (credentials), definitions (input/output schemas via TypeBox), and registers handler functions. Supports .getCredential() for secret access and .register() for function binding.

apiSurface.name: IConfigDefinition
apiSurface.description: From '@osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget'. Defines the configuration schema for Workshop custom widgets, specifying field types (string, number, boolean, date, timestamp, objectSet, lists) and input/output/event bindings.

apiSurface.name: foundry.config.json
apiSurface.description: Configuration file for Foundry website hosting via '@osdk/cli'. Contains foundryUrl, site.application (RID), site.directory (build output), and site.autoVersion settings for deployment.

apiSurface.name: Instance.content.fetchContents
apiSurface.description: Fetches media content from a MediaSet-backed property on an OSDK object instance. Returns a Blob that can be converted to a download URL via URL.createObjectURL().

architecturePatterns.name: Marketplace Package Distribution
architecturePatterns.description: Projects are packaged as .zip marketplace bundles via DevOps, containing Ontology definitions, backing datasets, Actions, Functions, and optional application code. Users upload to their enrollment's marketplace and install through a 4-stage process (General Setup, Input Configuration, Content Review, Validation). Packages can include language model inputs (GPT-4o, embeddings) that map to enrollment-specific models.

architecturePatterns.name: OSDK Client + Generated SDK Pattern
architecturePatterns.description: Every OSDK application follows the same initialization pattern: import createClient from '@osdk/client', import $ontologyRid from project-specific SDK package (e.g., '@whaletail/sdk'), create OAuth auth via createPublicOauthClient, then create client with (url, $ontologyRid, auth). The client is callable as a function: client(ObjectType) returns an ObjectSet.

architecturePatterns.name: Optimistic Action Updates with Undo
architecturePatterns.description: Foundry for Tunes implements optimistic UI updates: before calling applyAction, it patches the local React Query cache via $updateInternalValues, captures previous values, and returns an undo function. On action failure, the undo function restores previous values. This provides instant UI feedback while maintaining consistency.

architecturePatterns.name: React Query + OSDK Integration
architecturePatterns.description: Multiple projects use @tanstack/react-query with OSDK. Query functions use asyncIter() or fetchPage() to load objects, with queryKey-based caching. After action calls, invalidateQueries triggers refetching. Custom hooks (useObject, useSongs, usePlaylists) abstract the OSDK+React Query integration.

architecturePatterns.name: Compute Module as Server-Side Extension
architecturePatterns.description: Compute Modules provide server-side execution within Foundry. They use Docker containers with defined input/output schemas (TypeBox), can access secrets via getCredential(), and integrate with external APIs (Gmail, Geocoding). They support both pipeline execution mode (data transforms) and function execution mode (OSDK-callable functions).

architecturePatterns.name: Workshop Custom Widget via IFrame
architecturePatterns.description: Custom Workshop widgets are React apps hosted on Foundry, embedded via iframe. They use @osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget to define bidirectional data bindings (strings, numbers, booleans, dates, objectSets, events) between Workshop variables and the widget. Deployed via @osdk/cli with foundry.config.json.

architecturePatterns.name: Push-Based Event Streaming
architecturePatterns.description: Data Connection listeners accept webhooks from external systems (e.g., Jira Cloud) into Foundry streaming datasets. Listeners handle system-specific authentication (message signing), provision URLs for external system configuration, and feed real-time data that can be processed into the Ontology or monitored via Automate.

architecturePatterns.name: Platform SDK for Metadata Governance
architecturePatterns.description: The foundry-platform-sdk Conda package provides Python access to Foundry metadata (users, groups, resources, permissions). Authenticated via OAuth service user credentials stored in REST Data Connection sources. Transforms pull metadata into datasets that back governance Ontology objects.

typeDefinitions.name: Osdk.Instance<O>
typeDefinitions.definition: type Instance<O extends ObjectOrInterfaceDefinition> = { $primaryKey: string; $title: string; [property: string]: any; $updateInternalValues(update: Partial<Instance<O>>): void; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Typed OSDK object instance with all Ontology-defined properties, special meta-properties ($primaryKey, $title), and internal mutation support for optimistic updates.

typeDefinitions.name: ObjectSet<O>
typeDefinitions.definition: interface ObjectSet<O extends ObjectOrInterfaceDefinition> { where(filter): ObjectSet<O>; fetchPage(opts): Promise<{ data: Instance<O>[]; nextPageToken?: string }>; asyncIter(): AsyncIterable<Instance<O>>; aggregate(opts): Promise<AggregateResult>; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Composable query builder for Ontology objects supporting filtering, pagination, async iteration, and aggregation.

typeDefinitions.name: Client
typeDefinitions.definition: interface Client { (objectType: ObjectTypeDefinition): ObjectSet<O>; (actionType: ActionTypeDefinition): { applyAction(params, opts?): Promise<ActionResult> }; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Callable OSDK client that creates ObjectSets when called with Object Types and action executors when called with Action Types.

typeDefinitions.name: IConfigDefinition
typeDefinitions.definition: type IConfigDefinition = Array<{ fieldId: string; field: { type: 'single'; label: string; fieldValue: { type: 'inputOutput' | 'event'; variableType?: { type: 'string' | 'number' | 'boolean' | 'date' | 'timestamp' | 'objectSet' | 'string-list' | 'number-list' | ...; defaultValue?: any; objectTypeId?: string } } } }>
typeDefinitions.purpose: Schema definition for Workshop iframe custom widget configuration, defining typed bidirectional bindings between Workshop variables and widget state.

typeDefinitions.name: ComputeModule
typeDefinitions.definition: class ComputeModule { constructor(config: { logger; sources: Record<string, { credentials: string[] }>; definitions: Record<string, { input: TSchema; output: TSchema }> }); register(name: string, handler: (context) => Promise<any>): void; getCredential(source: string, key: string): Promise<string>; on(event: string, handler): this; }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Server-side compute execution runtime for Foundry Compute Modules. Manages credential access, function registration with typed schemas, and lifecycle events.

typeDefinitions.name: WhaleResponse (paginated)
typeDefinitions.definition: interface WhaleResponse { nextPageToken?: string; data: Array<{ commonName?: string; locality?: string; waterBody?: string; scientificName?: string; $primaryKey?: string; $title?: string; [prop: string]: any }> }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Paginated response shape returned by ObjectSet.fetchPage(), containing a page of typed object data and an optional continuation token.

typeDefinitions.name: AggregateResult (grouped)
typeDefinitions.definition: type AggregateResult = Array<{ $group: { [groupByProperty: string]: string }; $count: number }> | { $count: number }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Aggregation result shape: array of group-count pairs when using $groupBy, or single count object for ungrouped aggregations.

typeDefinitions.name: foundry.config.json schema
typeDefinitions.definition: { foundryUrl: string; site: { application: string; directory: string; autoVersion: { type: 'git-describe'; tagPrefix: string } } }
typeDefinitions.purpose: Deployment configuration for Foundry website hosting. Specifies the target enrollment URL, application RID, build output directory, and versioning strategy.

dependencies.name: @osdk/client
dependencies.purpose: Core OSDK client library for interacting with Foundry Ontology. Provides createClient, ObjectSet, and typed instance access.

dependencies.name: @osdk/oauth
dependencies.purpose: OAuth authentication library for OSDK. Provides createPublicOauthClient for browser-based OAuth flows with Foundry.

dependencies.name: @osdk/cli
dependencies.purpose: CLI tool for deploying OSDK applications to Foundry website hosting. Uses foundry.config.json for configuration.

dependencies.name: @osdk/foundry.admin
dependencies.purpose: Foundry admin SDK for accessing platform administration features.

dependencies.name: @osdk/workshop-iframe-custom-widget
dependencies.purpose: Library for building Workshop custom widgets via iframe, providing typed bidirectional data bindings.

dependencies.name: @palantir/compute-module
dependencies.purpose: Runtime library for Foundry Compute Modules. Provides credential management, function registration, and execution lifecycle.

dependencies.name: foundry-platform-sdk
dependencies.purpose: Python Conda package for accessing Foundry platform metadata (users, groups, resources, permissions) via the Platform SDK.

dependencies.name: @tanstack/react-query
dependencies.purpose: Data fetching and caching library used with OSDK for managing server state in React applications.

dependencies.name: @sinclair/typebox
dependencies.purpose: JSON Schema type builder used for defining Compute Module input/output schemas.

dependencies.name: react
dependencies.purpose: UI framework used by all OSDK frontend applications in the registry.

dependencies.name: vite
dependencies.purpose: Build tool and dev server used by all React-based OSDK applications.
